W1 capability-breadth first slice: getFoodCostSummary promoted pending→READY (now 9 ready agent tools = 5 AUTO + 4 CONFIRM; guests/sales still pending). Read-only, access-checked adapter (C-1 caller→location gate via userLocations/{uid}/{loc} or locations/{loc}/ownerId, fail-closed — a cross-tenant unit test proves an attacker gets {hasData:false}) reads the denormalised summary already persisted at locations/{loc}/stockUsage (no calculator extraction) → pure summariseFoodCost returns cost%, trend, low-stock-by-days-of-cover. Built brainstorm → 3-agent adversarial review (caught base order-calculator is LIVE not dead; calc-purity overstated — UI entry does an async RTDB read; O4 already answered by code → zero extraction; 2 CRITICAL security reqs) → spec v2 → subagent-driven TDD (4 tasks). 165/165 + build green; review folded (orderByKey().limitToLast(20) read bound; ESM→CJS test fix). Bundles the design + plan docs. Operator: live npm run eval q-foodcost. Deferred: D2 calculator → suggestedOrder; D3 v2 UI + CSV mapping-memory; D4 strip-and-harden sweep.

CRIT-07 gen2 fix + LIVE-ENFORCED in prod (#164). The #162 monitor soak caught a real valid:false on prod traffic: gen2 Cloud Functions strip the function-name path (req.originalUrl='/'), so reconstruction yielded '.../' while Twilio signs the full '.../receiveWhatsAppMessage' -> HMAC mismatch. Fix: verifyTwilioSignature validates against an explicit TWILIO_WEBHOOK_URL (env or comma/space list, valid-if-any; multi-URL accept is safe since forging needs the token), falling back to reconstruction only when unset. +5 tests reproducing the exact gen2 failure (22/22); review folded (TWILIO_WEBHOOK_URL in WHATSAPP_BOT_SOP.md, single error-log per call). Then deployed + verified live: monitor -> valid:true -> TWILIO_SIGNATURE_MODE=enforce -> real message processed under mode:'enforce'. CRIT-07 fully closed in prod with ZERO downtime across the whole rollout.

CRIT-07 — Twilio webhook signature verification (#162). Closes the forge-a-guest-message vector on the live WhatsApp webhook: new functions/utils/twilio-signature.js verifies X-Twilio-Signature (HMAC-SHA1) via the official twilio.validateRequest using TWILIO_TOKEN. Scoped to the only LIVE handler (receiveWhatsAppMessageEnhanced — live-caller trace found the non-enhanced file + whatsappApp express block are dead). Staged rollout via TWILIO_SIGNATURE_MODE (monitor default → enforce → off) so a URL-reconstruction mismatch can't dark-out a live channel. 17 tests exercise the REAL validator with crypto-generated signatures; local security+code review + automated review all clean (folded comma-list-host hardening + 2 guard tests + WHATSAPP_BOT_SOP docs; declined a pure-CJS test rewrite — mixed import/require is the repo convention). DEPLOY-GATED: operator runs the monitor soak then flips to enforce. Spun off a PII-log redaction task + dead-code cleanup suggestion.

bugs.title: cleanupOldQueuesScheduled cron has never deployed — export clobbered by module.exports reassignment
bugs.notes: queueAnalytics.js:343 does exports.cleanupOldQueuesScheduled = onSchedule('every 24 hours', ...), but line 357's module.exports = {...} replaces the whole exports object (CommonJS), dropping that assignment. So require('./queueAnalytics').cleanupOldQueuesScheduled is undefined, index.js:2243 exports undefined, and firebase reports 'No function matches the filter' — never a deployable function. Consequence: automated daily queue pruning is NOT running (only the manual cleanupOldQueues HTTP fn works), compounding the unbounded-node retention bug. Found during the ledger deploy (106 deployed vs 107 exports). Fix: include cleanupOldQueuesScheduled in the module.exports object (or move the assignment after it). One-line merge + redeploy.

bugs.title: salesData .write root still auth != null (any authed user can create new salesData records)
bugs.notes: The H-2 work (PR #96) tightened salesData reads to admin + per-child owner, but the root .write remains auth != null. The per-child .validate guards overwriting an existing record, but any authenticated user can still CREATE new salesData records under arbitrary keys (no userId-match enforced on create). Low-impact (data pollution, not exfiltration) but inconsistent with the read lockdown. Fix: create-path .validate/.write requiring newData.child('userId').val() === auth.uid || admin. Surfaced as a NIT on already-merged code.

bugs.title: rewardsProcessor.js:646 modulo bias in generateFallbackCode
bugs.notes: crypto.randomBytes(6) then byte % 36 over-represents chars 0-3 by ~14% (256 % 36 = 4). Far better than the old Math.random() but not uniform for a security-relevant voucher fallback code. Fix: rejection sampling (while code.length<6: b=randomBytes(1)[0]; if b<252 code += chars[b%36]). ~0.09 extra bytes/code. Surfaced as a 🟡 on already-merged OWASP-era code.

bugs.title: Recurrence-engine gap: nextDueDate never advances when daily workflow goes uncompleted
bugs.notes: Operator noted on PR #72 preview: 'I see one workflow that hasn't been completed daily since it was created.' rossSubmitResponse auto-completes the run when all required tasks are done but does NOT advance the workflow's nextDueDate for the next cycle. A daily workflow created 10 days ago with no run completed reads as daysLate: 10 instead of rolling forward. Fix needs: (a) on run completion, advance nextDueDate by recurrence period; (b) decide policy when a cycle is fully missed (advance on next 'create run' attempt OR scheduled CF walks every workflow nightly). Touches rossSubmitResponse + possibly rossScheduledReminder. Backlog candidate for a dedicated sprint — recurrence semantics need product input.

bugs.title: Double-seed race on signup retry — rossSeedFirstWorkflow idempotency pre-check is not transactional
bugs.notes: A user double-clicking signup or hitting a network retry could pass the firstWorkflowSeededAt pre-check in both invocations and produce two seeded workflows. Mitigation options: (a) RTDB transaction() on the marker, OR (b) deterministic workflow ID per uid (e.g. seed_{uid}) so duplicate write is overwrite. Exposure small; consequence mild (duplicate workflow, no corruption).

bugs.title: Unbounded-node retention policy — in-app 'Clear X' buttons are time-bombs without scheduled pruning

Options: scheduled prune CF (chunked-delete pattern), TTL-on-write, or rollups. Needs product call on retention window per node.

security.summary: RETRACTION (2026-07-21): HIGH-08 (@grpc/grpc-js) was wrongly marked closed by the 2026-07-02 and 2026-07-05 scans - 1.12.6 is the last vulnerable version (advisory range >=1.12.0 <1.12.7), confirmed still open by live npm audit. It is back on the books. Reconciled live exposure: 11 Criticals + 11 Highs, nothing fixed since 2026-06-16. Third automated audit (2026-07-19) re-verified all 29 findings from 2026-06-15 against live code — all remain open except CRIT-07 (closed+enforced) — and surfaced 11 NEW findings (5 Critical, 3 High, 1 Medium, 2 informational/low): CRIT-08 guests RTDB cross-tenant PII leak, CRIT-09 receipts Storage exposure (extends CRIT-05), CRIT-10 forgeable userLocations breaking ROSS tenant isolation, CRIT-11 rewards self-approval bypass on creation, CRIT-12 Meraki shared secret logged in plaintext (supersedes MED-09), plus HIGH-09/10/11 and MED-10. Logged only — read-only audit, no code changes. CRIT-07 (Twilio webhook HMAC forgery) remains CLOSED + LIVE-ENFORCED in prod. Prior audit (2026-05-30) fully remediated across 14 PRs (#89–#102). RTDB rules batch now also covers CRIT-08/09/10/11 + HIGH-09 + MED-10 — full writer census required before any rule tightening ships.
remaining: CRIT-08 — guests RTDB parent .read: auth != null cascades past the $phoneNumber-scoped child rule — any authenticated user (incl. a WiFi guest) can read every tenant's full guest list (database.rules.json:16-24) — assigned: claude-opus-4-8
CRIT-09 — receipts Storage path /receipts/{file} read: auth != null with no ownership scoping, extends CRIT-05 to raw receipt images (storage.rules:15-17) — assigned: claude-opus-4-8

CRIT-10 — userLocations self-writable with no ownership .validate — forges the sole trust primitive behind ROSS's verifyLocationAccess, enabling cross-tenant IDOR on all location-scoped ROSS CFs (database.rules.json:53-58, functions/ross.js:164-182) — assigned: claude-opus-4-8

CRIT-11 — rewards .write/.validate only enforce ownership/status on updates, not creation — any authenticated user can self-create a pre-approved arbitrary-value reward (database.rules.json:75-82) — assigned: claude-sonnet-4-6
CRIT-12 — Meraki webhook logs full req.body including the live shared secret in plaintext to Cloud Logging, supersedes MED-09 (functions/index.js:473) — assigned: claude-haiku-4-5

HIGH-09 — whatsapp-numbers/location-whatsapp-mapping parent write cascades past child ownership check, any authenticated user can hijack WhatsApp number-to-location routing (database.rules.json:325-344) — assigned: claude-sonnet-4-6 [DUPLICATE of NEW-CRIT-01 (2026-07-04): the same database.rules.json:325-344 write-cascade, found independently by two scans and rated Critical by one, High by the other. Count once, treat as Critical, close both IDs with one fix.]
HIGH-10 — triggerSubscriptionStatusCheck has no auth check at all (functions/subscriptionStatusManager.js:136-198) — assigned: claude-haiku-4-5

HIGH-11 — voucherService.js has no runTransaction() around pool assignment/fraud-check — double-spend race on concurrent redemption (functions/voucherService.js:21-99,337-383) — assigned: claude-sonnet-4-6

MED-10 — queues/queue-history/queue-states (the actual live QMS write paths) have no explicit RTDB rules at all, relies on implicit default-deny (database.rules.json, absent) — assigned: claude-haiku-4-5
INFO — Meraki secret comparison not constant-time (functions/index.js:467); FoodCostApp.vue:102 v-html on currently-static content, latent only (public/js/modules/food-cost/.../FoodCostApp.vue:102)

CRIT-07 — ✅ CLOSED + LIVE-ENFORCED in prod 2026-06-16 (#162 + #164). Both webhook functions deployed with TWILIO_SIGNATURE_MODE=enforce; real message verified (mode:'enforce', valid:true); forged requests now 403. #164 added TWILIO_WEBHOOK_URL for the gen2 path-strip. Zero downtime across the rollout.
